content.problemStatement:
Chef has a network consisting of NNN nodes labeled from 111 to totalNodestotalNodestotalNodes. Chef wants to send a signal starting from a specific node called startNodestartNodestartNode. The network is represented by a list of directed edges, where each edge describes the travel time of the signal from one node to another. 

Chef needs to determine how long it will take for the signal to reach every node in the network. If it is impossible for the signal to reach all nodes, Chef wants to know that as well.

Help Chef by calculating the minimum time required for the signal to propagate to all nodes starting from startNodestartNodestartNode. If any node cannot be reached, return −1-1−1.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecalculateNetworkDelayTimecalculateNetworkDelayTimecalculateNetworkDelayTime

Parameters
travelTimestravelTimestravelTimes : A list of travel times where each element is a vector of three integers `[source, target, time]` representing a directed edge and the travel time.
totalNodestotalNodestotalNodes : The total number of nodes in the network, labeled from 111 to totalNodestotalNodestotalNodes.
startNodestartNodestartNode : The node from which Chef sends the signal.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the minimum time it takes for all nodes to receive the signal.
Returns −1-1−1 if it is impossible for the signal to reach all nodes.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤totalNodes≤1001 \leq totalNodes \leq 1001≤totalNodes≤100
1≤Connections≤60001 \leq Connections \leq 60001≤Connections≤6000
1≤source, target≤totalNodes1 \leq \text{source, target} \leq totalNodes1≤source, target≤totalNodes
source≠targetsource \neq targetsource=target
0≤time≤1000 \leq time \leq 1000≤time≤100
All pairs (source,target)(source, target)(source,target) are unique (no multiple edges between the same nodes).

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of:

The first line each contain three integers sourcesourcesource, targettargettarget, and timetimetime describing a directed edge and its travel time.
The second line contains the NNN nodes - total number of nodes.
The last line of each test case contains a single integer startNodestartNodestartNode — the node from which the signal is sent.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer — the minimum time it takes for the signal to reach all nodes in the network starting from startNodestartNodestartNode. If it is impossible for all nodes to receive the signal, print −1-1−1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1
The network has two nodes: 1 and 2.

The signal starts from node 2.
Node 2 can send the signal to node 1 in 3 units of time.
Node 2 itself is reached at time 0.

So, the time taken for the signal to reach:

Node 2 -> 0
Node 1 -> 3

The signal reaches all nodes, and the maximum time taken is 3.
Output: 3

Test Case 2
The network contains three nodes: 1, 2, and 3.

The signal starts from node 1.
It reaches node 2 in 1 unit of time.
From node 2, it reaches node 3 in an additional 2 units of time.

So, the arrival times are:

Node 1 -> 0
Node 2 -> 1
Node 3 -> 3

All nodes are reachable, and the longest time taken for the signal to reach any node is 3.
Output: 3

Test Case 3
The network again has three nodes: 1, 2, and 3.


The signal starts from node 1.


Node 2 is reached in 1 unit of time.


Node 3 can be reached in two ways:

Directly from node 1 in 2 units of time.
Via node 2 in 1 + 2 = 3 units of time.



The shortest time to reach node 3 is 2.


So, the arrival times are:

Node 1 -> 0
Node 2 -> 1
Node 3 -> 2

The signal reaches all nodes, and the maximum time taken is 2.
Output: 2
